title: breynia quadrangularis
intro: Breynia quadrangularis, also known as the blackfruit vine, is a perennial climber native to Australia and New Guinea, characterized by its four-angled stems and shiny black berries. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Breynia quadrangularis include the potential treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a diuretic and laxative. Herbal preparations of Breynia quadrangularis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
